element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns steering and acceleration against 3 opponents
Collect to increase top speed temporarily
Protects from crash damage when hazards introduced
Fire forward to disable opponents; requires ammunition management
Fire backward to disable trailing opponents
Spin cars out of control; requires spatial awareness
Slow car; combined with AI aggression
Collect all 8 letters to upgrade car; provides strategic collection goal
Each upgrade increases speed and handling
Decrease ammo supply; forces weapon conservation strategy
Stationary speed boosts placed strategically on track

typical_relief_triggers: Using missiles to disable threatening opponent
Collecting roll cage before hazard section
Strategic use of zippers for speed boost
typical_tension_triggers: Approaching lap final sections with multiple opponents
Encountering clustered hazards (oil slicks + rain + barriers)
Yellow car AI acceleration to 127 mph (unbeatable threshold)
Track repeats with increased AI aggression (tracks 25+)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-relief across 32-track structure with escalating difficulty; early tracks introduce mechanics safely, mid tracks combine hazards with weapons, late tracks (25+) become punishing with max-speed AI opponents requiring constant weapon usage

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus letter placement and power-up distribution creates exploration incentive within racing path; collecting all 8 letters is optional but enables car upgrades

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces core mechanics (steering, acceleration, basic opponents) in gentle curve; 3 opponents manageable without heavy weapon reliance; tune-up items abundant
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Hazards multiply (oil slicks, rain, barriers); weapon economy becomes critical; opponent aggression increases; player must balance speed tuning with defensive roll cages
difficulty_curve.late_game: Tracks 25-32 repeat with escalated features; by track 32, all opponents run maximum speed (127 mph) and become unbeatable without constant missile/bomb use; game becomes attrition-based where eventual ammunition depletion leads to elimination
notable_spikes: Track 25+ restart cycle: dramatic jump in AI opponent speed
Track 32: all opponents at maximum speed threshold
Hazard introduction points where oil slicks combine with rain
Late game weapon scarcity: skull collection depletes ammo reserves

notable_level_patterns.description: Early tracks (1-8) focus on single mechanic introduction without hazard clusters; racing-first, with safe pickup collection
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Track 1-5: basic steering vs. 3 opponents, minimal hazards

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid tracks (9-24) layer hazards incrementally while maintaining weapon viability; oil slicks, rain, and barriers appear in distributed patterns rather than dense clusters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Track 10-20: combined oil/rain sections with clear racing line alternatives

notable_level_patterns.description: Tracks 25-32 repeat layout from tracks 1-8 but with maximum AI speed and mandatory weapon usage; same track geometry becomes survival challenge
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Track 25 is Track 1 layout with unbeatable AI opponents

notable_level_patterns.description: Late-game structure emphasizes ammunition scarcity; collecting skulls reduces ammo, forcing players to choose between weapon spending and shield vulnerability
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tracks 28-32: skull frequency increases, missile ammunition critical
